/resources.title: BdMSO Preparation: Syllabus, Regulations & Sample Questions
/resources.description: Syllabus, regulations, marks distribution, sample questions and parent guides to prepare for the BdMSO National Olympiad and the IMSO qualifying pathway.

mainEntity.name: Who is eligible to participate in BdMSO?

acceptedAnswer.text: BdMSO is open to students across Bangladesh who are 13 years old or younger, born on or after October 1, 2013, and enrolled in Grade 6 or lower as of October 1, 2026. Students in the British curriculum (or similar streams) who are promoted to Grade 7 in July/August 2026 are not eligible.

mainEntity.name: What is the format of the Olympiad?

acceptedAnswer.text: The BdMSO National Olympiad is a Math + Science exam with 60 minutes allocated per subject (50 + 50 = 100 marks total). It is held at multiple regional venues, simultaneously or on different dates. The day includes the National Anthem, the exam, a lunch break, a cultural program, and a prize-giving ceremony.

mainEntity.name: How do I register and what is the fee?

acceptedAnswer.text: Registration is online at www.bdmso.org. The Olympiad registration fee is 1000 BDT per student. Subsequent stages have separate fees: National Residential Camp 3,000–5,000 BDT, TST 1,000 BDT per subject, and the international participation fee is announced after the TST.

Approximately the top 20–30% of participants are designated Winners and receive a medal and Certificate of Achievement. Winners are classified as All-Round Winners (top 10% in both Mathematics and Science independently), Mathematics Winners (top 10% in Mathematics), or Science Winners (top 10% in Science). A Champion Trophy is awarded to the highest scorer in each section, presented on the final day of the National Residential Camp. Every participant also receives a Participant T-shirt, Certificate of Participation, ID, Badge, Sticker, and Snacks. Lunch is provided to participants at the Dhaka and Chattogram venues only.
Yes. The organizing committee may consider partial waivers of up to 50% on Olympiad, Camp, or TST fees for gifted students with significant financial need. Independent sponsorship is also encouraged.

mainEntity.name: How are IMSO team members selected?

acceptedAnswer.text: Olympiad winners are invited to a three-day National Residential Camp. About a week after the camp, the Team Selection Test (TST) - a day-long evaluation per subject - is held. The primary National Team is determined by the top six students per subject based on a weighted average of their National Round, Camp performance, and TST scores. The 12 students (6 Math + 6 Science) plus 2 Tutors and 1 Team Leader form the standard 15-member delegation.

mainEntity.name: What is IMSO and how does BdMSO connect to it?

acceptedAnswer.text: IMSO (International Mathematics and Science Olympiad) is a prestigious annual competition for primary school students under 13 from around the world. BdMSO is Bangladesh's official national qualifying round for IMSO. The national team selected through BdMSO will represent Bangladesh at the 23rd IMSO in 2026.

mainEntity.name: What are the costs for IMSO participation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Selected national delegates pay a registration fee covering travel, visa, accommodation, and local transport. One parent may accompany their child for an additional fee of approximately $650 USD payable directly to the IMSO organizers.

Follow the official BdMSO syllabus. It is recommended to practice elementary-level creative problems and study IMSO sample questions and past papers. Elementary-level Mathematics and Science textbooks are also useful.

mainEntity.name: How can I get further assistance?

acceptedAnswer.text: Email us at support@bdmso.org or visit our Facebook page at facebook.com/imsobd.
